summary: CC-0091 closes the openbao-finalizer vs ESO PushSecret adoption race by adding a Pass-0 adoption gate to finalizeOpenBaoSecrets: a backup PushSecret that is still live (non-Terminating) but does not yet carry ESO's cleanup finalizer (pushsecret.externalsecrets.external-secrets.io/finalizer) causes the handler to record SecretsReady=False/WaitingForESOAdoption and return (done=false, nil) without firing Delete — preventing the operator's Delete from outracing ESO's first reconcile and orphaning kv-v2 paths in OpenBao. Pass-1 (parallel Delete) and Pass-2 (wait-for-gone / OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked) are unchanged; Terminating PushSecrets skip Pass-0 (DeletionTimestamp != 0 short-circuit) so user-initiated finalizer stripping cannot re-trap the CR. The fix is implemented in operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets.go with three small additions: the esoPushSecretFinalizer constant, the hasESOFinalizer helper, and the setOpenBaoWaitingForESOAdoptionCondition helper. Unit tests in reconcile_secrets_test.go cover every Pass-0 branch (no adoption, full adoption, mixed state, Terminating, absent, RBAC-shape) and an envtest integration test (TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteRacingESOAdoption) walks the full racing-delete → adoption → cleanup lifecycle. The chainsaw step-6 bao kv check is wrapped in a bounded 30 s / 3 s retry loop so residual DeleteSecret latency after PushSecret GC does not re-flake the E2E. Docs (docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md) gain a Two-stage lifecycle subsection, a condition-reason reference table entry for WaitingForESOAdoption, and a narrative of the motivating race. RBAC is untouched — the existing pushsecrets verbs cover get+delete.

tests_checklist.item: Unit tests exist for every Pass-0 branch (unadopted, adopted, mixed, Terminating, absent, RBAC verb shape)
Interceptor-based Delete-count assertion proves Pass-0 fires ZERO Delete calls when any PushSecret is unadopted (mixed state)
Integration test TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteRacingESOAdoption exercises the full racing-delete → adoption → cleanup lifecycle under envtest
Existing TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_DeletesBothPushSecrets and TestIntegration_OpenBaoFinalizer_BlockedWhenPushSecretStuck still pass after helper refactor
Tests use named-finder helpers for container/resource lookup, not brittle index-based assertions
Condition message strings grep-discoverable (stable 'Waiting for ESO to adopt PushSecret' prefix)
No tautological constant tests remain (external review pattern reject-tautological-constant-tests)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows sub-reconciler condition contract: Type/Status/ObservedGeneration/Reason/Message fields all set (pattern: sub-reconciler-condition-contract-with-observedgeneration)
Constants centralized as single source of truth (esoPushSecretFinalizer mirrors keystoneOpenBaoFinalizer pattern)
hasESOFinalizer helper is internal to the package and covered by its own table-driven test
Pass-0 short-circuits on first unadopted PushSecret (no Delete side-effect before the condition is recorded)
Function doc comment updated to describe the new three-pass flow and name REQ-001/REQ-003/REQ-007
RBAC unchanged — existing get+delete on pushsecrets already covers Pass-0 Get + Pass-1 Delete
No dead code, no commented-out alternatives, no TODO/FIXME left behind

security_checklist.item: No new RBAC verbs or apiGroups granted (sync-rbac-markers-with-helm-clusterrole-templates: N/A — zero RBAC delta)
No secrets logged — only PushSecret object names surface in conditions and V(1) structured logs
Adoption check uses constant-string equality; no user-controlled input reaches the comparison
Pass-0 cannot be bypassed by an attacker who strips finalizers — a cleared ESO finalizer on a non-Terminating PushSecret keeps the CR Terminating indefinitely (fail-safe)
Terminating short-circuit allows operator-initiated unblock (strip finalizer after Pass-1) without re-trapping the CR

architecture_checklist.item: Minimal surgical change — Pass-0 inserted into finalizeOpenBaoSecrets, two helpers added, no new reconciler, no new CRD fields
Both backup PushSecrets handled symmetrically via openBaoBackupPushSecretNames (no per-resource branches)
New WaitingForESOAdoption reason is distinct from OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked — SREs can tell pre-Delete from post-Delete stalls from kubectl describe alone
Three-pass cleanup design is a natural extension of CC-0079's two-pass design — ordering invariants preserved
No scope creep — only files listed in the affected-areas section of the plan are touched

dry_yagni_checklist.item: esoPushSecretFinalizer declared once as constant; usage via named reference in handler, helper, and interceptor tests
hasESOFinalizer helper reused by the Pass-0 gate — not inlined
backupPushSecret vs backupPushSecretUnadopted split cleanly expresses the two fixture shapes needed by the tests (adopted default; unadopted for Pass-0 cases)
TestESOFinalizerConstant duplicates the literal value of the constant it asserts — tautological (see issue below)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Pass-0 returns on the FIRST unadopted PushSecret — no accumulation, no best-effort continuation
Non-NotFound Get errors in Pass-0 propagate (%w-wrapped) so controller-runtime retries with backoff
RequeueSecretPolling (15 s) requeue set by reconcileDeleteOpenBao on (done=false, nil)

defensive_checklist.item: NotFound tolerance preserved throughout Pass-0, Pass-1, Pass-2 for idempotency
DeletionTimestamp != 0 short-circuit prevents re-trapping a CR when a user manually strips the ESO finalizer
hasESOFinalizer handles nil/empty finalizer list (nil slice iteration is safe in Go)

issues_found.description: TestESOFinalizerConstant is a tautological test that hard-codes the literal string 'pushsecret.externalsecrets.external-secrets.io/finalizer' and asserts the package constant equals that literal. Two independent reasons to remove it: (1) the explicit blocker-class review criterion states the literal must appear exactly once in the repository (in reconcile_secrets.go) — this test duplicates the literal, violating that criterion; (2) the reject-tautological-constant-tests external-review pattern (severity WARNING, drawn from CC-0067 and CC-0078 where gndrmnn rejected TestKeystoneOpenBaoFinalizerConstant for the same reason) calls out this exact anti-pattern: 'Tests whose body is a single equality assertion between a package constant and a hardcoded literal, providing no behavioral coverage.' Any real drift would be caught by TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_BlocksOnMissingESOAdoption and the integration test (which seed/read the constant and would fail on a rename), so this test adds no coverage a real regression cannot already produce.
issues_found.fix: Delete TestESOFinalizerConstant (the whole function, lines 1378-1386 including its godoc). The behavioural tests (TestHasESOFinalizer, TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_BlocksOnMissingESOAdoption, TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteRacingESOAdoption) already cover any silent rename of esoPushSecretFinalizer because they exercise the constant through the Pass-0 gate against real ESO-shaped PushSecret objects.

issues_found.description: The new WaitingForESOAdoption docs section states 'No explicit RequeueAfter is set — the condition write triggers a watch-driven requeue, and controller-runtime's exponential backoff covers longer stalls.' This is incorrect: reconcileDeleteOpenBao (keystone_controller.go:388) returns ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: RequeueSecretPolling} on (done=false, nil), where RequeueSecretPolling is 15 s (requeue_intervals.go:22). So the adoption wait is actively polled every 15 s, not watch-driven. A reader triaging a stuck Terminating Keystone would walk away with an inaccurate mental model of the retry cadence.
issues_found.fix: Replace 'No explicit RequeueAfter is set — the condition write triggers a watch-driven requeue, and controller-runtime's exponential backoff covers longer stalls.' with 'reconcileDeleteOpenBao requeues every RequeueSecretPolling (15 s) while Pass-0 blocks, so ESO workqueue backlogs resolve on the next 15 s tick without operator intervention.' (or drop the reuqueue paragraph entirely and let the existing SecretsReady=False/OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked section handle it — the two states share the same caller requeue behaviour).
issues_found.evidence:
docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md:1021-1023: "primary observability signal. No explicit `RequeueAfter` is set — the
   condition write triggers a watch-driven requeue, and controller-runtime's
   exponential backoff covers longer stalls."
keystone_controller.go:387-389: "	if !done {
		return ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: RequeueSecretPolling}, nil
	}"
requeue_intervals.go:22: "	RequeueSecretPolling = 15 * time.Second"

issues_found.description: Section heading 'Two-stage lifecycle' (docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md:937) introduces a section whose body describes THREE numbered passes (Pass-0 adoption wait, Pass-1 parallel Delete, Pass-2 wait-for-gone). The heading's 'Two-stage' is meant to refer to the two observable BLOCKED states (pre-Delete and post-Delete), but the intro sentence and the numbered list both talk about three passes, creating a pre-read/post-read mismatch. Readers encountering the heading in the TOC expect two stages, then find three.
issues_found.fix: Rename the section to 'Three-pass cleanup with two blocked states' or similar, OR keep 'Two-stage lifecycle' and reword the body to collapse the three passes into two stages (pre-Delete adoption wait = stage 1; post-Delete parallel-Delete-plus-wait-for-gone = stage 2). The current wording is internally inconsistent.
issues_found.evidence:
docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md:937: "### Two-stage lifecycle"
docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md:950: "The stages execute in strict order:

1. **Pass-0 — adoption wait.** ...
2. **Pass-1 — parallel Delete.** ...
3. **Pass-2 — wait-for-gone.** ..."

suggested_improvements: Consider whether the Pass-0 wait creates event-emission noise: reconcileDeleteOpenBao emits FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets whenever hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets is true (DeletionTimestamp == 0 on any backup). During a prolonged Pass-0 wait the PushSecrets remain non-Terminating, so the event fires on every 15 s requeue until ESO adopts. Kubernetes event aggregation dedupes this in practice, but a future adopter of the Pass-0 pattern may want an explicit once-per-termination gate for the adoption-wait case.
next_steps: Delete TestESOFinalizerConstant (reconcile_secrets_test.go:1378-1386) to satisfy the 'literal appears exactly once' blocker criterion and align with the reject-tautological-constant-tests review pattern.
Fix the inaccurate requeue claim in the WaitingForESOAdoption docs subsection (keystone-reconciler.md:1021-1026).
Reconcile the 'Two-stage lifecycle' heading with the three-pass numbered list in its body.
